name: Kindergarten Drawing & Illustration — Semester 2
description: An 18-week, play-based Kindergarten pathway for characters, settings, picture stories, and revision. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.
learningGoals: Build confidence and curiosity with characters, settings, picture stories, and revision.
Communicate learning through talk, drawing, movement, play, or a simple creation.
Practice safe, kind, and responsible participation.

lessons.title: Notice and talk: characters, settings, picture stories, and revision
lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about characters, settings, picture stories, and revision through a close look at characters, settings, picture stories, and revision.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about characters, settings, picture stories, and revision through a guided practice with characters, settings, picture stories, and revision.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about characters, settings, picture stories, and revision through a small creation or demonstration about characters, settings, picture stories, and revision.

resources.title: National Core Arts Standards

units.title: Wonder and notice: characters, settings, picture stories, and revision
units.essentialQuestion: What can we notice and wonder about characters, settings, picture stories, and revision?
outcomes: I can notice details and share a question about characters, settings, picture stories, and revision.
I can use words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Notice and talk: characters, settings, picture stories, and revision
lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about characters, settings, picture stories, and revision through a close look at characters, settings, picture stories, and revision.

activities.title: A close look at characters, settings, picture stories, and revision
activities.learnerAction: With an adult or teacher nearby when needed, the learner participates in a close look at characters, settings, picture stories, and revision, uses words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate thinking, and cleans up safely.

successCriteria: The learner participates safely and respectfully in characters, settings, picture stories, and revision.
The learner communicates one observation, choice, or question using speech, drawing, movement, or a supported response.
